channel.title: NPR Topics: News
channel.description: NPR news, audio, and podcasts. Coverage of breaking stories, national and world news, politics, business, science, technology, and extended coverage of major national and world events.
channel.copyright: Copyright 2024 NPR - For Personal Use Only

item.title: U.S. and Iran to hold nuclear talks. And, Harvard professor resigns over Epstein ties
item.description: U.S. and Iranian officials are set to meet today in Geneva to discuss Tehran's nuclear program. And, Harvard professor Larry Summers is resigning over ties to convicted sex offender Jeffrey Epstein.
item.content:encoded:
U.S. and Iranian officials are set to meet today in Geneva to discuss Tehran's nuclear program. And, Harvard professor Larry Summers is resigning over ties to convicted sex offender Jeffrey Epstein.

(Image credit: Costas Metaxakis)
item.dc:creator: Brittney Melton

item.title: Pentagon shifts toward maintaining ties to Scouting
item.description: Months after NPR reported on the Pentagon's efforts to sever ties with Scouting America, efforts to maintain the partnership have new momentum
item.content:encoded:
Months after NPR reported on the Pentagon's efforts to sever ties with Scouting America, efforts to maintain the partnership have new momentum

(Image credit: David Ryder)
item.dc:creator: Graham Smith

item.title: Why farmers in California are backing a giant solar farm
item.description: Many farmers have had to fallow land as a state law comes into effect limiting their access to water. There's now a push to develop some of that land … into solar farms.
item.content:encoded:
Many farmers have had to fallow land as a state law comes into effect limiting their access to water. There's now a push to develop some of that land … into solar farms.

(Image credit: Jae C. Hong)
item.dc:creator: Dan Charles

item.title: Every business wants your review. What's with the feedback frenzy?
item.description: Customers want to read reviews and businesses need reviews to attract customers. But the constant demand for reviews could be creating a feedback backlash, experts say.
item.content:encoded:
Customers want to read reviews and businesses need reviews to attract customers. But the constant demand for reviews could be creating a feedback backlash, experts say.

(Image credit: Alicia Zheng)
item.dc:creator: James Doubek

item.title: Can't get a prescription renewed? Here's how to cope with prior authorizations
item.description: These health care hurdles can stand in the way of getting treatment your doctor says you need. Here's what to know about how to deal with them.
item.content:encoded: These health care hurdles can stand in the way of getting treatment your doctor says you need. Here's what to know about how to deal with them.
item.dc:creator: Sarah Boden

item.title: Tariffs cost American shoppers. They're unlikely to get that money back
item.description: After the Supreme Court declared the emergency tariffs illegal, the refund process will be messy and will go to businesses first.
item.content:encoded:
After the Supreme Court declared the emergency tariffs illegal, the refund process will be messy and will go to businesses first.

(Image credit: John Locher)
item.dc:creator: Stephan Bisaha

item.title: 'Get back to integrity': Oklahoma's Kevin Stitt on Republicans after Trump
item.description: NPR's Steve Inskeep asks Oklahoma Gov. Kevin Stitt about his spat with President Trump, immigration and the future of the Republican Party.
item.content:encoded: NPR's Steve Inskeep asks Oklahoma Gov. Kevin Stitt about his spat with President Trump, immigration and the future of the Republican Party.
item.dc:creator: Steve Inskeep

item.title: Civil rights leaders say the racial progress Jesse Jackson fought for is under threat
item.description: Activists say racial progress won by the Rev. Jesse Jackson is under threat, as a new generation of leaders works to preserve hard-fought civil rights gains.
item.content:encoded:
Activists say racial progress won by the Rev. Jesse Jackson is under threat, as a new generation of leaders works to preserve hard-fought civil rights gains.

(Image credit: Scott Olson)
item.dc:creator: Debbie Elliott

item.title: Morning news brief
item.description: U.S. and Iran to hold third round of nuclear talks, Harvard professor to retire amid school's investigation into his Epstein ties, Cuba says four killed on boat were trying to infiltrate country.
item.content:encoded: U.S. and Iran to hold third round of nuclear talks, Harvard professor to retire amid school's investigation into his Epstein ties, Cuba says four killed on boat were trying to infiltrate country.
item.dc:creator: Michel Martin

item.title: Harvard professor Larry Summers to resign as school investigates his ties to Epstein
item.description: Harvard professor and economist Larry Summers will resign at the end of the academic year amid the school's on-going investigation into his relationship with Jeffrey Epstein.
item.content:encoded: Harvard professor and economist Larry Summers will resign at the end of the academic year amid the school's on-going investigation into his relationship with Jeffrey Epstein.
item.dc:creator: Suevon Lee
